scores.brief_assessment: A well-structured, highly actionable skill rich with proprietary Grove/Bluehawk conventions unavailable in training data; its main weaknesses are moderate redundancy (partial-file-includes guidance repeated in both Step 7 and Edge Cases) and occasional hedged guidance ('prefer placing in an existing directory when suitable') where a more prescriptive rule would serve better.
scores.novel_info: The most novel elements are the internal "Grove" test suite framework itself — its directory layout, the proprietary `Expect API` method signatures (`shouldMatch`, `shouldResemble().withSchema()`), the custom test wrappers (`describeWithSampleData` / `itWithSampleData`), and the internal reference files (`references/page-scanning.md`, `references/data-adaptation.md`, `conventions-{language}.md`) that the skill depends on but whose contents are not shown. A reviewer should also fact-check the `literalinclude :category:` value taxonomy (e.g., `usage example`, `non-mongodb command`, `configuration object`) and the Bluehawk tag conventions (`:replace-start:`, `:uncomment:`) as used within Grove, since both are described with specificity that goes beyond Bluehawk's public documentation.
